title: Executive Order 9011—Prescribing Regulations Governing the Manner of Expending and Accounting for Funds Appropriated for the Army of the Philippines

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Commanding General, USAFFE, is granted broad, self-determined authority over Philippine-Treasury-funded wartime expenditures 'for such purposes as he may deem proper,' issued amid the active Japanese invasion of the Philippines that began December 8, 1941.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Explicit accountability-removing language: the Commanding General's determination 'shall be final and conclusive upon the accounting officers of the Philippine Government' — an express removal of an existing independent audit check.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'For such purposes as he may deem proper' is an undefined, self-determined standard.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The same 'final and conclusive' language removes the pre-existing Philippine accounting officers' independent review authority.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Issued at the height of the Japanese invasion and siege of the Philippines (the Bataan/Corregidor campaign), this funding-and-accounting flexibility was existentially significant for the besieged USAFFE garrison — genuine historical consequence beyond a routine administrative order.

title: Executive Order 9012—Possession Relinquished of Plant of Federal Shipbuilding & Dry Dock Company

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A deregulatory reversion action: relinquishes Navy possession of the plant seized under EO 8868, per the genuine disclosed termination standard already built into that order and now triggered by the President's determination that private operation is consistent with defense needs — reduces rather than creates structural weight.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed factual basis for the reversion determination.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Restores rather than removes oversight/ownership.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight

title: Executive Order 9018—Suspension of the Provisions of the Act of March 3, 1931, as to the War and Navy Departments and the Coast Guard

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Suspends a statutory hours-of-work protection for War/Navy Department and Coast Guard civilian personnel, granting the Secretaries self-determined regulatory authority over working hours for the duration of the emergency.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed check preserved for DC-area employees: hours regulations are 'subject...to the coordination and approval of the Director of the Bureau of the Budget.'

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Tied to 'the period of the existing national emergency,' with no independent expiration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The Bureau of the Budget coordination/approval requirement provides a genuine, disclosed check, though limited to DC-area employees.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Part of the broader wartime pattern of rolling back federal civilian working-condition protections, with a genuine, disclosed effect on covered employees' statutory hours protections.

title: Executive Order 9019—Revoking in Part and Modifying Executive Order No. 8578 of October 29, 1940, and Reserving Public Land for Use of the War Department as an Aerial Machine-Gun Range

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Very large (over 3 million combined acres) but genuinely well-designed, protective bombing/gunnery-range withdrawal (Nevada), diff-aware modification of EO 8578.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Extensive, genuinely disclosed carve-outs: preserves four separate pre-existing wildlife/water-reserve/grazing-district orders 'so far as such orders affect' the land, and explicitly authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to renegotiate boundaries with the Secretary of War.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Mandates local Army commandant consultation with Fish and Wildlife Service and Grazing Service representatives, designates guaranteed non-firing access days, and states explicit intent to return the land to Interior administration when no longer needed.

title: Executive Order 9023—Extension of the Provisions of Executive Order No. 9001 of December 27, 1941, to Contracts of the Treasury Department, the Department of Agriculture, the Federal Works Agency, the Panama Canal, the Government Printing Office, and the National Advisory Committee for Aeronautics

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Extends EO 9001's broad wartime contract-modification, advance-payment, and requirement-waiver authority ('without regard to the provisions of law relating to the making, performance, amendment, or modification of contracts') to six additional civilian agencies.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Inherits EO 9001's disclosed check structure by reference: mandatory quarterly reporting to the President, non-discrimination clause, and Assignment of Claims Act preservation.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Inherits EO 9001's self-determined 'in the judgment of' standards for contract modifications and claim settlements.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Inherits EO 9001's notably rich disclosed check structure: mandatory quarterly Presidential reporting (with a disclosed restricted/confidential carve-out procedure), preservation of the Walsh-Healey, Davis-Bacon, Copeland, and Eight-Hour Acts, a 7%-of-cost fixed-fee cap, a no-kickback contractor warranty, and an explicit prohibition on cost-plus-a-percentage-of-cost contracting.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Extends the systemic wartime procurement-flexibility apparatus (originating in the December 18, 1941 'Act to Expedite the Prosecution of the War Effort') government-wide across six more civilian agencies.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: A coordinative extension of an existing mechanism to additional agencies, not a subordination of one agency's authority to another.

title: Executive Order 9024—Establishing the War Production Board in the Executive Office of the President and Defining Its Functions and Duties

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Chairman is granted 'general direction over the war procurement and production program' and authority to 'determine the policies, plans, procedures, and methods' of ALL federal departments, establishments, and agencies with respect to war procurement and production, issuing binding directives — one of the single most sweeping concentrations of civilian economic authority in the entire corpus, now absorbing the already-CRITICAL SPAB's functions and supervisory authority over OPM into one office.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Board membership (Secretaries of War and Navy, Federal Loan Administrator, OPM Director General and Associate Director General, OPA Administrator, Board of Economic Warfare Chairman, and defense-aid Special Assistant) is disclosed, but the order specifies the Chairman acts only 'with the advice and assistance of' the Board — an advisory, non-binding structure paralleling EO 8985's Censorship Policy Board.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single coherent subject (direction of the wartime production/procurement economy), despite absorbing multiple predecessor institutional functions.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As he may deem necessary or appropriate' is the operative, self-determined standard governing the Chairman's directive-issuing authority.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Explicit, unambiguous textual finality clause: 'his decisions shall be final' — among the most extreme preemption-of-review provisions in the entire corpus — combined with unilateral authority to 'direct such changes in [OPM's] organization as he may deem necessary,' i.e., authority to unilaterally restructure another federal agency.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: The War Production Board became the central civilian institution directing the entire American wartime industrial economy for the duration of World War II — one of the most consequential and enduring institutional developments in this corpus, comparable in historical weight to the Selective Service System.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Abolishes the already-CRITICAL-rated Supply Priorities and Allocations Board outright, transferring its personnel, records, and property, and asserts direct supervisory and reorganization authority over the Office of Production Management — the single most explicit and complete instance of one office absorbing and restructuring pre-existing institutions found anywhere in this corpus, formally amending or superseding six named predecessor orders (EO 8629, 8875, 8891, 8942, 9001, 9023) in the process.

title: Executive Order 9048—Suspending the Limitations Upon Punishments for Violations of Articles of War 58, 59 and 86

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Removes existing maximum-punishment limitations for desertion, aiding/advising desertion, and misbehavior of sentinels, broadening courts-martial punishment discretion for offenses committed after this date.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Implements existing Article of War 45 authority to modify the Table of Maximum Punishments, a lawful pre-existing mechanism.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: 'Until further order' — open-ended, with no fixed expiration.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Genuine wartime consequence for individual servicemembers: removing punishment caps for desertion-related offenses in the opening months of the war materially increases exposure to severe courts-martial sentences, including potential capital punishment for desertion.

title: Executive Order 9049—Ordering Certain Organizations and Units of the Organized Reserves Into the Active Military Service of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed statutory basis.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed bound: 'for the duration of the present war and for six months after the termination thereof, subject to earlier relief or discharge.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; implements explicit statutory authorization.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically significant in scale: activates the entire remaining Organized Reserves of the United States into active service, a major full-mobilization event in the weeks following Pearl Harbor.

title: Executive Order 9054—Establishing a War Shipping Administration in the Executive Office of the President and Defining Its Functions and Duties

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Administrator is granted control over the operation, purchase, charter, requisition, and use of virtually the entire U.S.-flag and U.S.-controlled ocean shipping fleet (narrow combat-vessel and coastwise-shipping carve-outs aside), and Section 8 grants the same extreme finality language found in EO 9024 (War Production Board): 'his decisions shall be final with respect to the functions and authorities so vested in him.'

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Section 2(g) requires the Administrator to 'keep the President informed with regard to the progress made,' a real but modest disclosed reporting mechanism that does not functionally constrain day-to-day final-decision authority.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single coherent subject (wartime ocean shipping control).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As he may deem necessary or appropriate' is the operative, self-determined standard for the Administrator's directive-issuing authority.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: A genuine, disclosed, substantive external check exists but is narrow in scope: for war-production-cargo movement priorities specifically, the Administrator 'shall be guided by schedules transmitted to him by the Chairman of the War Production Board' — a real subordination to another (already CRITICAL) institution, though this covers only one function among many otherwise governed by the Administrator's own final, unchecked decisions.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: The War Shipping Administration became the central wartime authority over the entire American and Allied-controlled merchant marine, directly enabling Battle of the Atlantic logistics and the trans-oceanic supply chain underpinning the entire Allied war effort — extraordinarily consequential.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Transfers ALL of the U.S. Maritime Commission's core statutory vessel-operation, purchase, charter, insurance, repair, and requisition functions to itself — an explicit, complete cannibalization of another agency's authority, tempered somewhat by the disclosed WPB cargo-priority guidance requirement and mandated liaison with War, Navy, and ODT.

title: Executive Order 9055—Extension of the Provisions of Executive Order No. 9001 of December 27, 1941, to Contracts of the Interior Department

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Extends EO 9001's broad wartime contract-modification, advance-payment, and requirement-waiver authority to the Department of the Interior, matching the treatment given the identical EO 9023 extension.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Inherits EO 9001's disclosed check structure by reference (quarterly Presidential reporting, non-discrimination clause, Assignment of Claims Act preservation).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Inherits EO 9001's self-determined 'in the judgment of' standards.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Inherits EO 9001's rich disclosed check structure (quarterly Presidential reporting, Walsh-Healey/Davis-Bacon/Copeland Act preservation, 7% fee cap, no-kickback warranty, cost-plus-percentage prohibition).

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Extends the systemic wartime procurement-flexibility apparatus to another major civilian department.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: A coordinative extension of an existing mechanism, not a subordination of one agency's authority to another.

title: Executive Order 9056—Exemption of August G. Ahlfont From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard one-year retirement-age exemption template.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed position, fixed one-year term.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Fixed one-year end date (March 31, 1943) — bounded.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9057—Amending Regulations Concerning Foreign Service Pay Adjustment

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Purely technical currency-exchange-rate table update for a specific set of territories, diff-aware supersession of EO 8800.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed rate figures.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion created.

title: Executive Order 9058—Extension of the Provisions of Executive Order No. 9001 of December 27, 1941, to Contracts of the Tennessee Valley Authority

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Extends EO 9001's broad wartime contract-modification, advance-payment, and requirement-waiver authority to the Tennessee Valley Authority, matching the treatment given the identical EO 9023/9055 extensions.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Inherits EO 9001's disclosed check structure by reference.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Inherits EO 9001's self-determined 'in the judgment of' standards.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Inherits EO 9001's rich disclosed check structure.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Extends the systemic wartime procurement-flexibility apparatus to another major federal instrumentality.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: A coordinative extension of an existing mechanism, not a subordination of one agency's authority to another.

title: Executive Order 9059—Excluding Certain Tracts of Land From the Chugach and Tongass National Forests and Restoring Them to Entry

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A deregulatory, beneficial action restoring dozens of small pre-existing homesite parcels (Alaska) to private-entry eligibility for their occupants — reduces rather than creates structural weight.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed, itemized list of specific homesite parcels and surveys.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Removes a restriction rather than creating unchecked discretion.

title: Executive Order 9060—Including Certain Lands in the Fremont National Forest

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard land-classification/administrative-transfer order (Oregon) implementing a 1934 boundary-revision statute, subject to valid existing rights and pre-existing power/reclamation withdrawals.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed legal description and statutory basis.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9061—Placing Certain Lands Within the Fremont National Forest Under the Administration of the Department of the Interior

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard inter-agency administrative-transfer order (Oregon) implementing the existing Taylor Grazing Act framework, subject to valid existing rights.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed legal description and statutory basis.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9062—Exemption of Michael Conley From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard one-year retirement-age exemption template.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed position, fixed one-year term.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Fixed one-year end date (February 28, 1943) — bounded.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9063—Authorizing the Civil Service Commission To Adopt Special Procedures Relating to Recruitment, Placement, and Changes in Status of Personnel for the Federal Service

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Civil Service Commission — itself the government's civil-service oversight body, a structurally different and less concerning locus than a typical unilateral line-official grant — is authorized to adopt binding 'special procedures' government-wide (except postal field service) 'as it may determine to be necessary' for wartime recruitment, placement, and status changes.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Ultimate retention/status discretion for appointees hired under these special procedures remains expressly 'in the discretion of the Civil Service Commission' itself, the oversight body.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As it may determine to be necessary' is a vague, self-determined standard with no further disclosed criteria.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed sunset: appointees under special procedures may be retained only 'for the duration of the war and for six months thereafter.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Authority is vested in and retained by the Civil Service Commission itself, the pre-existing independent oversight body, rather than ceded to an unchecked executive official.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically significant: authorizes wholesale, government-wide bypass of ordinary competitive-examination hiring procedures for the duration of the war, a foundational structural shift enabling the massive wartime expansion of the federal civilian workforce.

flags.name: Exemptions Architecture
flags.justification: A broad, government-wide exemption from regular competitive-examination and classified-status rules — a systemic exemptions-architecture event, though tempered relative to EO 8534/9004 by its genuine disclosed war-plus-six-months sunset.

title: Executive Order 9064—Authorizing the Governor of the Panama Canal To Furnish Certain Transportation to Persons Engaged for Service on the Isthmus of Panama

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, protective transportation/subsistence benefit for new Panama Canal employees.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed dollar cap ($6/day subsistence) and scope.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed sunset: effective only 'during the continuance of the present war and for six months after the termination thereof.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; genuinely beneficial in character.

title: Executive Order 9065—Amendment of Section 11 of the Regulations Governing Highways, Vehicles, and Vehicular Traffic in the Canal Zone

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Authorizes the Canal Zone Governor to amend, modify, supplement, or suspend traffic regulations 'when in his judgment such action is necessary in the public interest' during war or when war is 'imminent' in the Governor's own opinion.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Narrow, geographically bounded to Canal Zone traffic regulations only; expressly made subject to the provisions of a disclosed cross-referenced order (EO 8232).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'In his judgment...necessary in the public interest' is a vague, self-determined standard.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Tied to the war or imminent-war period as determined by the Governor himself, with no independently fixed expiration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Narrowly bounded to Canal Zone traffic rules, and expressly subject to the provisions of the disclosed cross-referenced order.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Narrow, local in ultimate consequence (Canal Zone vehicular traffic regulation) rather than an independently significant structural development.

title: Executive Order 9067—Providing for the Transfer of Personnel to War Agencies

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Establishes a government-wide mechanism, driven by Bureau of the Budget self-determined agency 'priority classifications,' for reallocating federal civilian personnel toward higher-priority war agencies.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed adjudicative process: the losing agency receives 10 days' notice and an opportunity to present evidence that the transfer would jeopardize its work, with the Civil Service Commission making a considered 'final decision'; employee consent is expressly required for transfer.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Relative importance to the war program' is a self-determined Bureau of the Budget standard with no further disclosed criteria.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed evidentiary process for the losing agency and a required employee-consent protection are both preserved, meaningfully constraining unilateral transfer.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable as the systematic mechanism enabling the large-scale wartime reallocation of federal civilian personnel toward war-priority agencies (feeding the growth of WPB, WSA, OPA, and others), diff-aware supersession of conflicting provisions of EO 8973.

title: Executive Order 9068—Partial Revocation of Executive Order No. 6795 of July 26, 1934, Withdrawing Public Lands

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Routine housekeeping partial revocation pending resurvey (Wyoming).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed effective-date trigger (plat filing).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9069—Consolidating Certain Agencies Within the Department of Agriculture

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Internal departmental consolidation of overlapping/related existing bureaus and agencies (Surplus Marketing Administration, Agricultural Marketing Service, Commodity Exchange Administration, AAA, Soil Conservation Service, Federal Crop Insurance Corporation, Sugar Division, and multiple research bureaus) under the continuing, accountable direction of the Secretary of Agriculture, implementing explicit First War Powers Act reorganization authority — administrative efficiency consolidation, not a grant of new unchecked power.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed statutory basis; Bureau of the Budget approval required for fund transfers.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed sunset: 'shall remain in force during the continuance of the present war and for six months after termination thereof.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Implements explicit congressional War Powers Act delegation; the Secretary of Agriculture remains the accountable official throughout.

title: Executive Order 9070—Consolidating the Housing Agencies and Functions of the Government Into the National Housing Agency

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Consolidates five previously separate agencies/boards (FHA, Federal Home Loan Bank Board, HOLC, FSLIC, USHA) plus defense-housing functions from three additional departments under one Administrator's 'direction and supervision,' with broad self-determined authority to centralize administrative services — though notably the Administrator position is Senate-confirmed, unlike most contemporaneous OEM-style wartime directorships.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Senate confirmation for the Administrator is a genuine, disclosed check largely absent from comparable 1942 institution-creation orders, but the three subordinate Commissioners and extensive delegated administrative discretion beneath the Administrator are not independently confirmed.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single coherent subject (federal housing policy) despite absorbing many predecessor bodies.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Detailed, specifically enumerated transfers across seventeen lettered subsections, not left to a vague standard.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Duration tied to 'so long as Title I of the First War Powers Act...remains in force' — open-ended but statutorily anchored, with no fixed calendar expiration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Senate confirmation requirement provides a genuine check; Section 13 explicitly preserves pre-existing contractual obligations and the United States Housing Act's annual-contribution pledges; extensive disclosed fund-transfer procedures require Bureau of the Budget approval.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically significant: the National Housing Agency was a major, enduring consolidation of federal housing policy that shaped the institutional lineage eventually leading to HUD.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Abolishes the Central Housing Committee outright and absorbs five previously independent agencies/boards into a single hierarchy — a substantial cannibalization event, tempered by the genuine disclosed Senate-confirmation check at the Administrator level.

title: Executive Order 9071—Transferring Functions of the Federal Loan Agency to the Department of Commerce

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Pure transfer of the Federal Loan Agency's remaining functions (RFC, Export-Import Bank, and eight subsidiary corporations) to an existing, Senate-confirmed, accountable Cabinet department (Commerce) — companion order to EO 9070, but without creating a new institutional layer.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed transfer with genuine Bureau of the Budget oversight of fund transfers.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed termination tied to the First War Powers Act Title I's own duration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The Secretary of Commerce is an existing, Senate-confirmed, accountable official; no new unchecked authority is created.

title: Executive Order 9072—Transferring Certain Vessels and Personnel From the Coast and Geodetic Survey to the War and Navy Departments

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, named transfer of specific vessels and individually-listed officers to War/Navy jurisdiction, matching the established EO 9007 treatment.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individuals and vessels, disclosed statutory basis.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed automatic-reversion clause: vessels and officers 'shall be returned to the Coast and Geodetic Survey when the present national emergency ceases to exist.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; automatic-reversion mechanism preserved.

title: Executive Order 9073—Extending the Limits of the Customs Port of Entry of Detroit, Michigan, in Customs Collection District Number 38 (Michigan)

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Routine administrative boundary adjustment of a customs port's geographic limits.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed list of included municipalities/townships.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9074—Directing the Secretary of the Navy to Take Action Necessary to Protect Vessels, Harbors, Ports, and Waterfront Facilities

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Broad, self-determined authority for the Secretary of the Navy to 'take such steps, institute such measures, and issue such regulations and orders as shall be necessary' to protect vessels, harbors, ports, and waterfront facilities nationwide (plus Alaska, Hawaii, Puerto Rico, and the Virgin Islands), diff-aware modification of EO 8972.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No compensation, reporting, or independent review mechanism is disclosed for the exercise of this broad protective authority.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As shall be necessary' is an undefined, self-determined standard.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to national defense, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed non-preemption clause explicitly preserving the FBI's existing sabotage/espionage investigative jurisdiction, matching the established EO 8972 precedent.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically significant given the active German U-boat campaign against U.S. Atlantic shipping in early 1942 (the 'Second Happy Time'), issued at a moment of acute waterfront-security anxiety.

title: Executive Order 9075—Authorizing and Directing the Secretary of War to Issue Citations in the Name of the President of the United States to Army Units for Outstanding Performance in Action

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Purely honorary/commendatory authority (unit citations, streamers, insignia), matching the established EO 9050 treatment.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed criteria and procedure.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9076—Authorizing Inspection by the Office of Price Administration of Corporation Statistical Transcript Cards Prepared From Income and Declared-Value Excess-Profits Tax Returns

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical inter-agency information-sharing authorization for a defined historical tax-year range, subject to Treasury-prescribed regulations.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed check: inspection must comply with Treasury Decision regulations approved the same date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed Treasury-regulation check preserved.

title: Executive Order 9077—Exemption of Gustave R. A. Elmgren From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard one-year retirement-age exemption template.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed position, fixed one-year term.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Fixed one-year end date (February 28, 1943) — bounded.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9078—Establishing the Army Specialist Corps

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Secretary of War granted authority to appoint an entire new uniformed civilian corps of War Department specialists, bypassing normal competitive-examination requirements government-wide for these positions.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed grade-floor check (Civil Service Commission agreement required for positions below Grade P&S-2/CAF-7), and recruiting responsibility is vested in the Civil Service Commission itself; genuine disclosed voluntary employee-consent and reinstatement-rights protections for transferees (Section 6).

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Bypasses competitive examination for Corps appointments; appointees do not acquire classified civil-service status.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed sunset: 'shall remain in force during the continuance of the present war and for six months after the termination thereof.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed CSC recruiting role and grade-floor check meaningfully constrain the scope of the bypass.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable as a significant wartime civil-service bypass mechanism specifically addressing the War Department's technical/specialist staffing needs, covering potentially thousands of positions.

flags.name: Exemptions Architecture
flags.justification: A substantial, though war-bounded, exemptions-architecture creation specifically carving War Department specialist positions out of normal civil-service status and classification.

title: Executive Order 9079—Making Certain Public Health Service Hospitals Available for the Care and Treatment of Insane Persons

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, protective administrative arrangement relieving overcrowding at St. Elizabeth's Hospital by using existing Public Health Service hospital capacity, implementing existing statutory authority plus First War Powers Act.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed reimbursement mechanism between the relevant appropriations.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed sunset: war plus six months.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; narrow, beneficial administrative purpose.

title: Executive Order 9080—Joint Mexican-United States Defense Commission

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Purely advisory bilateral planning commission (study problems, propose cooperative measures to the respective governments), no independent enforcement or regulatory authority — matches the established investigative/advisory-commission template.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named appointees, disclosed procedure, reports proposals back to the respective governments for their own action.

title: Executive Order 9081—Withdrawing Public Lands for Use of the War Department for Aviation Purposes

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard land-withdrawal template (Arizona, 2,062.72 acres, aviation purposes), subject to valid existing rights, diff-aware.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed legal description; genuine disclosed return-to-Interior provision when no longer needed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed reversion mechanism preserved.

title: Executive Order 9082—Reorganization of the Army of the United States and Transfer of Functions Within the War Department

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Purely internal military command-structure reorganization (the landmark creation of Army Ground Forces, Army Air Forces, and Services of Supply under their respective Commanding Generals), matching the established EO 8984 treatment for internal military C2 matters; genuine disclosed preservation of Presidential Commander-in-Chief authority 'through the Chief of Staff...in relation to strategy, tactics, and operations' (Section 6).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed transfer of specific named functions between named commands, implementing explicit First War Powers Act authority.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed sunset: war plus six months.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Internal military chain-of-command matter, not implicating civilian governance/accountability architecture; explicit disclosed preservation of civilian Commander-in-Chief authority over strategy.

title: Executive Order 9084—Amending Executive Order No. 8512 of August 13, 1940, Prescribing Regulations Pertaining to Budgetary Administration and Financial Reporting

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An oversight-strengthening amendment: newly requires that proposed uniform budgetary terminology/classifications be referred to the Comptroller General for conflict review before establishment, and gives the Comptroller General final determination authority if a conflict is found — adds a check rather than removing one.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed new Comptroller General referral requirement.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Adds oversight rather than removing it.

title: Executive Order 9085—Withdrawal of Public Land for Use as an Administrative Site by the Alaskan Fire Control Service

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard, small (80-acre) land-withdrawal template (Alaska, fire-control patrol station).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed legal description.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9086—Withdrawing Public Lands for Use of the War Department as a General Bombing Range

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard, large (184,371-acre) land-withdrawal template (Nevada), subject to valid existing rights.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed legal description; genuine disclosed preservation of a pre-existing grazing-district order.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed intent to return the land to Interior administration when no longer needed.

title: Executive Order 9088—Prescribing Regulations Concerning Civilian Defense

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Director of Civilian Defense is granted 'full discretionary authority' to define localities, allocate equipment/supplies/services under self-established priorities, and recall allocated resources, implementing the January 27, 1942 Civilian Defense Act.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed accountability mechanisms: mandatory 'full and accurate records' of all property (Section 7), a required War Department information-sharing/reporting relationship on procurement (Section 5), and civil-authority accountability conditions attached to every equipment loan (Section 11).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As he shall determine,' 'as he may deem advisable,' and similar self-determined standards govern allocation and locality-definition decisions throughout.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No explicit termination date or condition is disclosed within this order's own text.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed criminal-referral requirement for theft/misuse of civilian defense property (Section 10) and civil-authority use/protection/return conditions on all loans (Section 11) provide real, disclosed checks.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically significant as the nationwide civil-defense apparatus built in direct response to the acute West Coast invasion-scare and U-boat-threat anxiety of early 1942, the same period from which the Japanese-American exclusion orders would soon emerge.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: The War Department's procurement role is a disclosed cooperative service-provision relationship, not a subordination of its own authority.

title: Executive Order 9089—Prescribing Regulations Governing the Use, Control, and Closing of Stations and Facilities for Wire Communications

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Vests the Defense Communications Board with the President's full Section 606(d) authority to designate any wire-communications facility (telephone/telegraph) nationwide for use, control, supervision, inspection, or closure — the structural companion to the radio-control order (EO 8964), now issued with the U.S. formally at war with Japan, Germany, and Italy simultaneously.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Identical genuine disclosed checks to EO 8964: compensation requires Presidential approval (not self-assessment) and all regulations of general applicability must be published in the Federal Register.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single coherent regulatory domain (wartime wire-communications control).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'If the national security and defense and the successful conduct of the war so demand' is the same self-determined, undefined triggering standard as EO 8964.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing 'from and after this date,' tied to the war, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Same genuine disclosed checks as EO 8964: Presidential approval of compensation, mandatory Federal Register publication, and a disclosed inter-agency reporting requirement.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: The structural companion to EO 8964, activating full wartime federal control over the entire national wire (telephone/telegraph) communications infrastructure — comparably foundational to the radio-control order in enabling wartime coordination and security.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Coordinative rather than cannibalizing, implementing a specific Congressional delegation through the pre-existing Defense Communications Board.

title: Executive Order 9090—Establishing an Airspace Reservation Over Portions of Ulster and Dutchess Counties, New York

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard airspace-reservation template (New York), matching the established EO 8950/EO 8961 treatment — violators are 'subject to the penalties prescribed in the Civil Aeronautics Act,' existing statutory penalties are referenced rather than a self-contained coercive mechanism created.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed metes-and-bounds description.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new coercive enforcement mechanism created beyond existing statutory penalties.

title: Executive Order 9091—Establishing the Beltrami Wildlife Management Area

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Large (81,049-acre) but genuinely well-designed, protective land-jurisdiction transfer (Minnesota) from Agriculture to Interior for wildlife-refuge purposes, implementing existing Bankhead-Jones Act authority, subject to valid existing rights.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed preservation of a state cooperative-management license agreement and Agriculture's residual authority to complete pending land acquisitions.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Multiple genuine disclosed limitations and preserved third-party/state rights.

title: Executive Order 9092—Designating the Honorable A. Cecil Snyder as Acting Judge of the District Court of the United States for Puerto Rico

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, individual, temporary (current-calendar-year) judicial-coverage designation implementing existing statutory authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed narrow scope.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Bounded to the current calendar year.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9093—Certifying the Island of Puerto Rico as a Distressed Emergency Area

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, protective certification enabling expanded farm-loan limits under a pre-existing 1937 Act framework, matching the established EO 9006 (Hawaii) treatment.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed statutory basis and recommending officials.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Bounded to 'the continuance of the present war and for six months after termination thereof, or until such earlier time as I may designate.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; narrow, beneficial in effect.

title: Executive Order 9094—Abolishing the Board of Surveys and Maps and Authorizing the Director of the Office of Management and Budget to Perform Its Functions

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical consolidation of an inter-agency coordination board's function (federal surveying/mapping coordination) into the existing, accountable Director of the Bureau of the Budget; a modest information-request authority, not new coercive power.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed, narrow scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; narrow coordination function absorbed by an existing accountable official.

title: Executive Order 9095—Establishing the Office of Alien Property Custodian and Defining Its Functions and Duties

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Any property of any foreign country or national 'shall vest in the Alien Property Custodian whenever the Alien Property Custodian shall so direct' — an unqualified, wholly self-triggering unilateral property-VESTING (permanent confiscation, not merely blocking/freezing) authority, among the most extreme individual-official power grants in this corpus.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The Custodian must designate a liaison representative to the Board of Economic Warfare and operates 'subject to the provisions of Executive Order No. 8839,' a disclosed institutional tie-in, though this does not constrain the core vesting-trigger decision itself.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single coherent subject (enemy property administration).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Whenever the Alien Property Custodian shall so direct' discloses no criteria whatsoever for the vesting-trigger decision.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war/Trading with the Enemy Act framework, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No disclosed review, appeal, or check mechanism constrains the vesting-trigger decision itself; this office directly recreates the WWI-era Alien Property Custodian institution, which carries a well-documented historical record of corruption scandals stemming from precisely this kind of unchecked discretionary vesting authority.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: The WWII Office of Alien Property Custodian went on to administer billions of dollars in seized enemy assets, a historically consequential wartime institution recreating a predecessor office already notorious for governance controversy.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Carefully carves out and preserves the Secretary of the Treasury's and the Federal Reserve Board of Governors' already-delegated authority ('shall continue to be vested in and exercised by' them respectively) — a genuine, disclosed respect for existing institutional boundaries rather than cannibalizing them.

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Purely internal Navy Department command-structure reorganization (combining the Chief of Naval Operations and Commander in Chief, United States Fleet into one officer — the Admiral King dual-hatting arrangement), matching the established EO 8984 treatment for internal military C2 matters.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed staff structure and succession provisions.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Internal military administrative matter; the combined officer remains 'directly responsible, under the general direction of the Secretary of the Navy, to the President.'

flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9103—Providing Uniform Control Over the Publication and Use of Federal Statistical Information Which Would Give Aid and Comfort to the Enemy

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Director of the Bureau of the Budget granted broad, self-determined authority to determine whether government agency statistical publications comply with wartime information-security policy.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Applies only to the government's own statistical publications, not private or third-party speech; genuine disclosed carve-out preserving data access for authorized Federal officials.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Narrowly bounded to the government's own statistical releases, a materially narrower scope than the general communications-control orders (EO 8964/8985/9089).

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A component of the broader wartime information-control apparatus, though narrower in reach than the general censorship/communications-control orders.

title: Executive Order 9104—Withdrawing Public Lands for Use of the War Department as an Aerial Gunnery Range

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed intent to return the land to Interior administration when no longer needed.

title: Executive Order 9105—Amendment of Executive Order No. 8704 of March 4, 1941, Prescribing Regulations Governing the Granting of Allowances for Quarters and Subsistence to Enlisted Men

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, protective technical amendment adding advance-payment timing rules for enlisted personnel allowances.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed statutory basis and specific timing rules.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9106—Excepting Certain Persons From the Classification of "Alien Enemy" for the Purpose of Permitting Them to Apply for Naturalization

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Protective, beneficial delegation of the Attorney General's certification authority to except loyal alien enemies from the 'alien enemy' classification for naturalization purposes, implementing an explicit statutory framework (Nationality Act of 1940, Sec. 326(d)) requiring investigation 'fully establishing loyalty.'

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed statutory standard requiring DOJ investigation and certification; diff-aware supersession of the WWI-era EO 3008.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuinely protective/beneficial; the underlying statute itself preserves procedural safeguards (90-day notice, right to object) for affected individuals.

title: Executive Order 9107—Withdrawing Public Lands for Use of the War Department for Military Purposes

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard, small (1,241.40-acre) land-withdrawal template (California), subject to valid existing rights.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed legal description; genuine disclosed return-to-Interior provision when no longer needed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed reversion mechanism preserved.

title: Executive Order 9108—Directing the Director of the Office of Defense Transportation To Take Control of the Toledo, Peoria, and Western Railroad Company

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Directs ODT's Director to 'take immediate possession' of the railroad's entire property/franchise/assets and operate it 'in such manner as he deems necessary,' with authority to use 'the armed forces of the United States' if needed — the first railroad seizure order in this stream, extending the established plant-seizure template to critical transportation infrastructure.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Extensively disclosed factual chain matching the plant-seizure precedent: a certified labor dispute, an NWLB arbitration order under the Railway Labor Act, the company's refusal despite the employees' agreement and the President's urgent personal request.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed, substantive termination standard: possession continues 'only until the President determines that such temporary possession and operation are no longer required for successful prosecution of the war,' matching the EO 8773/8868/8928 precedent.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Followed a genuine, disclosed institutional escalation ladder (NWLB order, company refusal, Presidential request) rather than an ad hoc unchecked seizure; Section 3 explicitly preserves existing state and federal labor/health/safety laws for the railroad's employees.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable as an escalation of the wartime labor-dispute-resolution seizure pattern from factories to critical rail transportation infrastructure, part of the arc culminating in the 1943 Smith-Connally Act.

title: Executive Order 9109—Revoking in Part Executive Order No. 6583 of February 3, 1934, and Withdrawing Public Lands for Use of the War Department for Military Purposes

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard, small (1,280-acre) land-withdrawal template (New Mexico), diff-aware partial revocation of EO 6583, subject to valid existing rights.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed legal description; genuine disclosed return-to-Interior provision when no longer needed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed reversion mechanism preserved.

title: Executive Order 9110—Enlarging the Fort Gulick Military Reservation

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, small (8.64-acre) diff-aware boundary amendment to the already-scored EO 8737 (Fort Gulick Military Reservation, Canal Zone).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed metes-and-bounds description.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 8737.

title: Executive Order 9112—Authorizing Financing Contracts to Facilitate the Prosecution of the War

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: War Department, Navy Department, and Maritime Commission authorized, 'without regard to the provisions of law relating to...contracts,' to guarantee or make war-production loans through Federal Reserve Banks using a self-determined 'deemed...to be necessary, appropriate, or convenient' standard.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed reporting requirement incorporating EO 9001's Title II reporting regulations by reference.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Deemed...to be necessary, appropriate, or convenient' is a broad, self-determined standard.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: A real, substantial, disclosed external check: ALL Federal Reserve Bank actions under this authority 'shall be subject to the supervision of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System and to such directions and conditions as the Board of Governors...may prescribe' — an independent institutional check distinguishing this from less-checked wartime financing orders.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically significant as the mechanism (the wartime 'V-loan' program) that channeled large-scale production financing through the Federal Reserve System, notably facilitating small-business participation in war production.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Explicitly utilizes, rather than subordinates, the Federal Reserve System's existing infrastructure and expertise in a disclosed cooperative agency relationship, with genuine reimbursement provisions for the Reserve Banks' costs and losses.

title: Executive Order 9113—Transferring Certain Vessels and Personnel From the Coast and Geodetic Survey to the War and Navy Departments

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, named transfer of specific vessels and individually-listed officers to War/Navy jurisdiction, matching the established EO 9007/9072 treatment.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individuals and vessels, disclosed statutory basis.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed automatic-reversion clause: vessels and officers 'shall be returned to the Coast and Geodetic Survey when the present national emergency ceases to exist.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; automatic-reversion mechanism preserved.

title: Executive Order 9114—Withdrawing Public Lands for Use of the War Department for Military Purposes

flags.justification: Standard land-withdrawal template (Alaska, ~2,697 combined acres, Sitka), subject to valid existing rights, diff-aware partial revocation of a 1925 proclamation.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed metes-and-bounds description.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

flags.justification: Standard land-withdrawal template (New Mexico, 623.90 acres, Rio Grande Canalization Project), subject to valid existing rights, diff-aware supersession of EO 6910.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed legal description.

title: Executive Order 9116—Extension of the Provisions of Executive Order No. 9001 of December 27, 1941, to Contracts of the Office of the Coordinator of Inter-American Affairs, the Civil Aeronautics Administration, the National Housing Agency, the Veterans' Administration, and the Federal Communications Commission

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Extends EO 9001's broad wartime contract-modification/waiver authority to five more agencies, plus a narrower advertising-bid-waiver extension specifically to the FCC, matching the treatment given the identical EO 9023/9055/9058 extensions.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Inherits EO 9001's disclosed check structure by reference; a genuine disclosed scope-limiting proviso restricts applicability 'only to contracts relating to the prosecution of the war effort.'

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Inherits EO 9001's self-determined 'in the judgment of' standards.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Inherits EO 9001's rich disclosed check structure, plus the genuine disclosed war-effort-relevance limitation.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Extends the systemic wartime procurement-flexibility apparatus to five more agencies.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: A coordinative extension of an existing mechanism, not a subordination of one agency's authority to another.

title: Executive Order 9117—Prescribing Regulations Governing Overtime Compensation of Certain Employees of the National Advisory Committee for Aeronautics

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Protective/beneficial regulation granting overtime pay to essential NACA field employees, implementing a specific February 1942 Act; genuine disclosed hourly caps (max 48 hrs/week) and health/efficiency safeguard proviso.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed formula and criteria; Federal Register publication required.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed health/efficiency protective proviso and an explicit policy to 'hold overtime work to a minimum' (Section 4).

title: Executive Order 9118—Exemption of Harry L. Mickey From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard retirement-age exemption template, a further one-year extension of a prior exemption (EO 8698).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed position, fixed one-year term.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Fixed one-year end date (April 30, 1943) — bounded.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard, small (40-acre) wildlife-refuge enlargement template (Florida), subject to valid existing rights, diff-aware supersession of EO 6964.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed legal description.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight

flags.name: Power Concentration

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, protective technical amendment updating shipment/reimbursement procedures for federal employee relocations, including a genuine disclosed furlough-based extension for employees entering military service.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; genuinely protective/administrative in character.

title: Executive Order 9123—Authorizing the Procurement Division to Use Quartz Crystals Acquired Pursuant to the Act of June 7, 1939

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.
