flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed legal description and Secretary of Agriculture recommendation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9125—Defining Additional Functions, Duties and Powers of the War Production Board and the Office of Price Administration

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Extends the already-CRITICAL WPB Chairman's authority (EO 9024) into civilian rationing under Second War Powers Act Title III, delegated to the newly-created Office of Price Administration, with the same extreme finality clause: 'the decision of the Chairman of the War Production Board shall be final.'

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The Board's disclosed 'advice and assistance' role remains non-binding, matching EO 9024's structure.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single coherent subject (extending WPB/rationing authority to OPA).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As he may deem to be necessary or appropriate' is the operative self-determined standard.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Retains EO 9024's extreme finality clause and additionally grants the Price Administrator explicit subpoena-issuance authority plus independent civil-litigation authority to enforce rationing/priorities regulations in any Federal, State, or Territorial court — a substantial new investigatory/enforcement power with no disclosed independent check on its exercise.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Operationalizes the OPA civilian rationing regime that would govern gasoline, sugar, tires, and dozens of consumer goods for the duration of World War II, directly and continuously touching nearly every American household — one of the most historically memorable domestic wartime programs.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Explicitly channels authority into OPA as a coordinated, disclosed delegation from WPB rather than a cannibalization of an independent agency's pre-existing authority.

title: Executive Order 9126—Transferring Cognizance of the Duties and Functions of the Hydrographic Office and the Naval Observatory From the Bureau of Navigation, Navy Department, to the Chief of Naval Operations

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Purely internal Navy Department administrative reorganization, matching the established EO 8984/9096 treatment for internal military C2/administrative matters.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed transfer of specific named functions between named Navy offices.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Internal military administrative matter, not implicating civilian governance/accountability architecture.

title: Executive Order 9127—Designating the Departments and Agencies to Inspect the Plants and Audit the Books and Records of Defense Contractors Under Title XIII of the Second War Powers Act, 1942

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Grants six named agencies (WPB, War, Navy, Treasury, Maritime Commission, RFC) broad new investigatory authority to inspect defense contractors' plants and audit their books and records, coordinated under WPB Chairman governance.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed secrecy-classification procedure requirement for sensitive inspections (Section 3), and WPB Chairman's coordinating/governing role provides a check on inter-agency consistency.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Implements a specific statutory framework (Title XIII, Second War Powers Act) with a defined scope tied to the statutory 'defense contract' definition, not left vague.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed temporal bound: applies only to contracts placed 'after the declaration of emergency on September 8, 1939, and before the termination of the present war.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Grants subpoena-issuance authority (with Department of Justice court-enforcement backing) as a substantial new coercive investigatory tool, with no disclosed independent external review of its exercise beyond normal court subpoena-enforcement procedure.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically significant as part of the broader wartime profiteering-prevention and excess-profits-limitation apparatus shaping how WWII defense contractors were audited and overseen.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Explicitly coordinative among six named agencies under a disclosed WPB-governing framework, not a cannibalization of any single agency's authority.

title: Executive Order 9128—Defining Additional Functions and Duties of the Board of Economic Warfare

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Grants the Board of Economic Warfare (the already CRITICAL-rated EO 8839/8900 institution) broad new authority to 'determine the policies, plans, procedures, and methods' of federal agencies regarding imported war materials and civilian-economy commodities, mirroring the WPB Chairman's own domestic-authority language for the import side of the war economy.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed Presidential-approval check for the most consequential sub-authority: creation of new RFC-subsidiary financing corporations requires 'the approval of the President' (Section 1(b)).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As it may deem necessary' is the operative self-determined standard for directives and proposals.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed Presidential-approval check preserved for corporation creation; also explicitly excludes 'arms, munitions, or weapons of war' from BEW's import-materials authority, a genuine disclosed scope carve-out.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Continues the historically significant Economic Defense Board/Board of Economic Warfare institutional arc already scored as CRITICAL/high multiple times in this stream (EO 8839, EO 8900), diff-aware amendment of those predecessor orders plus EO 8926 and EO 8982.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Explicitly operates as the War Production Board's delegate/subordinate for import-materials coordination ('receive and be responsible for executing directives from the Chairman of the War Production Board') — a disclosed cooperative/subordinate relationship, not a cannibalization.

title: Executive Order 9129—Authorizing the United States Maritime Commission to Acquire and Dispose of Property

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Authorizes the Maritime Commission to acquire, use, and dispose of any real property (and personal property located thereon) that it 'shall deem necessary' for war purposes, implementing Second War Powers Act Title II.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Shall deem necessary' is a brief, self-determined standard with no further criteria disclosed in this order.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war (Second War Powers Act duration), with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A narrow, procedural property-acquisition mechanism implementing a specific Congressional Title, not independently a landmark development beyond the already-scored Maritime Commission/War Shipping Administration institutional family.

title: Executive Order 9130—Reserving Certain Public Lands in Connection with the Randolph County State Game Refuge

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard, small (280-acre) wildlife-refuge reservation template (Arkansas), cooperatively administered with the State Game and Fish Commission, subject to valid existing rights, diff-aware supersession of EO 6964.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed legal description and cooperative state-federal arrangement.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9131—Exemption of Leo A. McIntire From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard one-year retirement-age exemption template.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed position, fixed one-year term.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Fixed one-year end date (April 30, 1943) — bounded.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9132-A—Assignment of Frequencies to Government Radio Stations

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical spectrum-management matter limited to frequency assignment among GOVERNMENT radio stations (not a censorship or control power over private broadcasters).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: This order was classified CONFIDENTIAL and, per the official Presidential documents archive, 'was not received for publication in the Federal Register' — the only order encountered in this entire coding effort whose substantive text was never publicly disclosed, a genuine and notable transparency gap.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The classified, never-published status itself constitutes a preemption of any public or independently-checked oversight of this order's actual contents; full operative text is unavailable for detailed review.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Based on the disclosed title/scope, this appears to be a routine wartime government-spectrum-coordination matter rather than an independently landmark development; scored conservatively given the unavailability of full text.

title: Executive Order 9133—Transferring Certain Motor Repair Shops With Their Personnel and Property and the Functions of Operations and Maintenance Thereof From the Civilian Conservation Corps to the War Department

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Internal transfer of forty-five CCC motor repair shops to the existing, accountable War Department, implementing First War Powers Act reorganization authority; genuine disclosed cooperative-use provisions requiring the War Department to make shops available to other listed agencies (Sections 2-3).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed itemized list of transferred facilities; genuine disclosed reimbursement requirement for cross-agency repair services.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Transfer to an existing accountable Cabinet department; genuine disclosed cooperative-use safeguards preserved.

title: Executive Order 9134—Amendment of Executive Order No. 8757 of May 20, 1941 Establishing the Office of Civilian Defense

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Director of Civilian Defense granted broad, self-determined authority across seven enumerated functions, including authority to 'review and approve all civilian defense programs of Federal agencies involving the use of volunteer services' — a real coordinating/gatekeeping power reaching into other agencies' programs.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed Cabinet-level Civilian Defense Board (Secretary of War, Attorney General, Secretary of the Navy, and others) providing 'advice and assistance'; Presidential approval required for establishing the Office's principal subdivisions.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Functions are specifically enumerated across seven lettered subsections, not left to an undefined standard.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the declared state of war, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed Presidential-approval requirement for organizational structure and the Board's advisory role provide real, disclosed checks.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically significant civilian-defense apparatus, formalized amid the acute early-1942 West Coast invasion anxiety that also produced EO 9088.

title: Executive Order 9135—Establishing the Interdepartmental Committee for the Voluntary Pay Roll Savings Plan for the Purchase of War Savings Bonds

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Purely voluntary, coordinative committee facilitating opt-in payroll savings-bond purchase plans for federal employees; no coercive or regulatory authority created.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed membership (agency heads) and advisory/coordinating functions.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Purely voluntary program with no compulsory element.

title: Executive Order 9136—Modifying Executive Orders No. 1919½ of April 21, 1914, No. 2728 of October 8, 1917, and No. 3672 of May 8, 1922, and Reserving Public Lands for the Use of the War Department

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical road right-of-way reservation (Alaska, Fort Richardson), diff-aware modification of three old townsite orders.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed metes-and-bounds description; genuine disclosed preservation of Alaska Railroad's usage rights.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed preserved third-party rights.

title: Executive Order 9137—Withdrawing Public Lands for Use of the War Department for Flood Control Purposes

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard, small (805.34-acre) land-withdrawal template (Arkansas, Norfolk Dam), implementing a 1938 Act, subject to valid existing rights.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed legal description; diff-aware supersession of EO 6964.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9138—Providing Further for the Administration of the Requisitioning of Property Required for the Prosecution of the War

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow amendment to the already-CRITICAL property-requisitioning apparatus (EO 8942/9040) that further loosens it: explicitly REVOKES paragraph 4b(4)'s determination requirement, removing a disclosed procedural constraint that previously existed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Directly and explicitly revokes a disclosed procedural check ('the determination described in paragraph 4b(4)...shall not be required'; 'paragraph 4b(4)...is hereby revoked') — a rare instance of a subsequent order actively removing rather than adding a safeguard to an already-significant mechanism.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Continues and further streamlines the already historically significant wartime property-requisitioning institutional arc under the new Second War Powers Act Title VI authority.

title: Executive Order 9139—Establishing the War Manpower Commission in the Executive Office of the President and Transferring and Coordinating Certain Functions to Facilitate the Mobilization and Utilization of Manpower

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Chairman granted broad, self-determined authority to 'formulate...basic national policies' and 'direct the several departments and agencies...as to the proper allocation of available manpower,' issuing binding policy and operating directives across nearly a dozen named federal agencies plus 'all other' agencies performing manpower-related functions.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed multi-agency Commission structure (representatives of War, Navy, Agriculture, Labor, WPB, Selective Service, and CSC), with the Chairman required to act only 'after consultation with the members of the Commission.'

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single coherent subject (wartime manpower mobilization) despite absorbing multiple predecessor functions.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Basic national policies' and 'such policies, directives, regulations, and standards as the Chairman may prescribe' are broad, largely self-determined standards.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed multi-agency consultation requirement preserved; the order also explicitly preserves Selective Service's own core classification function rather than seizing it outright, requiring conformance only with respect to specific war-industry manpower coordination.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically very significant: the War Manpower Commission became the central WWII institution governing labor allocation and essential-worker deferment policy, directly touching millions of American workers.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Explicitly transfers several previously distinct functions (the WPB Labor Division's labor-supply function, the CSC's National Roster of Scientific and Specialized Personnel, the Office of Procurement and Assignment) directly into the new Commission, and requires roughly ten additional named agencies to conform to the Chairman's directives — a substantial, explicit multi-agency subordination event.

title: Executive Order 9140—Establishing the Safford National Wildlife Refuge

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard, small (240-acre) land-jurisdiction-transfer template (Arizona) implementing the Bankhead-Jones Act framework, subject to valid existing rights.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed legal description and Secretary of Agriculture recommendation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9141—Possession and Operation of Plants of Brewster Aeronautical Corporation

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Directs the Secretary of the Navy to 'take possession of and operate' Brewster Aeronautical's three plants, through 'such person or persons or instrumentality as he may designate,' and to exercise 'any existing contractual or other rights' of the company.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Unlike the established plant-seizure precedents (EO 8773/8868/8928/9108), this order discloses NO factual predicate or institutional escalation process (no NDMB certification, mediation record, or documented company refusal) — merely a bare assertion that seizure 'is deemed essential,' a notable accountability gap relative to the stream's usual seizure template.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed, substantive termination standard: possession ends when the President determines the plants 'will be privately operated in a manner consistent with the war effort.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Given the absence of any disclosed institutional escalation process (unlike the labor-dispute-driven seizures), this represents a more unilateral, less externally-checked seizure decision.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable: Brewster Aeronautical's chronic management and labor difficulties persisted throughout the war, eventually leading to a second, more famous Navy takeover in 1944.

title: Executive Order 9142—Transferring Certain Functions, Property, and Personnel From the Department of Justice to the Alien Property Custodian

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Pure consolidation/transfer of pre-existing DOJ Alien Property Division functions into the already-scored CRITICAL Alien Property Custodian institution (EO 9095) — not an independent new grant of unchecked authority beyond what was already captured in that scoring.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed checks preserved: the Attorney General retains supervisory authority over all Custodian-related litigation (Section 5), and personnel transfer without loss of civil-service status (Section 4).

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed sunset: war plus six months.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed non-preemption clause explicitly preserving FBI authority (Section 6), and DOJ litigation-supervision retention.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A consolidation/administrative-transfer order, not independently landmark beyond what is already captured in the EO 9095 scoring.

title: Executive Order 9143—Withdrawing Public Lands for Use of the War Department for Holding, Reconsignment, and Quartermaster Depots

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard, small (315-acre) land-withdrawal template (California), subject to valid existing rights and a power-project withdrawal, diff-aware.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed legal description; genuine disclosed return-to-Interior provision when no longer needed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed reversion mechanism preserved.

title: Executive Order 9144—Exemption of Herbert H. Evans From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard one-year retirement-age exemption template.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed position, fixed one-year term.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Fixed one-year end date (April 30, 1943) — bounded.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9145—Reserving Public Lands for the Use of the Alaska Road Commission in Connection With the Construction, Operation and Maintenance of the Palmer-Richardson Highway

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical highway right-of-way reservation (Alaska), diff-aware modification of four prior orders.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed survey/map reference and metes-and-bounds description.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9146—Authorizing the Secretary of the Interior to Withdraw and Reserve Public Lands

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Delegates a ministerial signing function (land withdrawal/reservation orders) to the Secretary of the Interior, but preserves an unusually rich, disclosed multi-layered check structure — arguably resulting in MORE oversight than direct Presidential signing, not less.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed checks: prior approval required from both the Director of the Bureau of the Budget AND the Attorney General (per the existing EO 7298 review process), mandatory Federal Register filing, and prior concurrence required from any other affected department or agency head.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: An unusually well-checked administrative delegation with multiple disclosed independent-review requirements preserved.

title: Executive Order 9147—Revocation of Executive Order No. 2987 of November 4, 1918, Placing Certain Land and Water Areas Under the Jurisdiction of the Secretary of Navy for Use as a Naval Air Station

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Routine deregulatory revocation returning Canal Zone land from Navy control to the Panama Canal Governor — reduces rather than creates structural weight.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed effect.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Removes a jurisdictional restriction rather than creating unchecked discretion.

title: Executive Order 9148—Transferring Credit Union Functions, Records, Property, and Personnel From the Farm Credit Administration to the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Pure inter-agency function transfer to an existing, accountable independent institution (FDIC), implementing First War Powers Act reorganization authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed statutory basis; genuine disclosed excess-personnel-retransfer safeguard.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed termination tied to First War Powers Act Title I's own duration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Transfer to an existing accountable institution; no new unchecked authority created.

title: Executive Order 9149—Amendment of Executive Order 5643 of June 8, 1931, Prescribing Regulations Governing Representation and Post Allowances

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An oversight-strengthening amendment: adds explicit disclosed prohibitions on representation-allowance use (vehicle expenses, club dues, printing) and newly requires competitive bidding for allowance-charged expenditures — adds checks rather than removing them.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed new prohibitions and competitive-bid requirement.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Adds oversight rather than removing it.

title: Executive Order 9150—Authorizing the Federal Public Housing Commissioner, National Housing Agency, To Acquire and Dispose of Property

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Authorizes the Federal Public Housing Commissioner to acquire, use, and dispose of real property 'deemed necessary' for war purposes under Second War Powers Act Title II, matching the treatment given the near-identical EO 9129 (Maritime Commission) authorization, though tempered by a genuine disclosed scope limitation restricting applicability 'only to property in connection with defense housing and temporary shelter.'

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Deemed necessary' remains a brief, self-determined standard, though the disclosed subject-matter scope limitation genuinely narrows its application.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war (Second War Powers Act duration), with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A narrow, procedural property-acquisition mechanism for a specifically bounded purpose (defense housing/temporary shelter), not independently a landmark development beyond the already-scored National Housing Agency institutional family.

title: Executive Order 9151—Transfer of Certain Public Land From Public Buildings Administration, Federal Works Agency, to the War Department

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard inter-agency land-jurisdiction-transfer order (Florida, Mullet Key) for a bombing range, with a disclosed historical chain of prior transfers back to 1882.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed legal description and historical chain of title/jurisdiction.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9152—Directions and Regulations Concerning Census Reports

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow authority to dispense with or curtail regular census/statistical work (notably suspending the 1941 manufacturing census) for war purposes, implementing Second War Powers Act Sec. 1401, with a genuine disclosed check explicitly stated twice: subject to 'the exercise by the Director of the Bureau of the Budget of his responsibilities for the coordination of Federal statistical activities.'

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed statutory basis and Budget Director coordination check.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Tempered by the twice-disclosed Bureau of the Budget coordination requirement.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine, explicitly disclosed Budget Director check preserved.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Narrow, procedural (affects only census-taking/statistical-reporting activities), not independently landmark.

title: Executive Order 9153-A—Withdrawing Public Lands for Use of the War Department for Military Purposes

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Title indicates a standard land-withdrawal-for-military-purposes template matching the dozens of near-identical orders scored throughout this stream; full operative text was unavailable, but the disclosed title and pattern strongly indicate the routine bounded template (subject to valid existing rights, narrow purpose-specific reservation).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Consistent with the standard template's disclosed legal-description format.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No indication of any departure from the standard, narrowly-bounded land-withdrawal template.

title: Executive Order 9154—Authorizing Certain Exclusions From the Operation of the Civil Service Retirement Act of May 29, 1930, as Amended

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical categorical exclusion of marginal/atypical employment categories (brief-service, part-time, fee-basis, piece-work, cooperative, nominal-compensation, and similar employees) from retirement-benefit ACT coverage, implementing a new January 1942 statute — an administrative-benefits eligibility matter, not a governance/enforcement-structure grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed enumerated categories (ten lettered classes); genuine disclosed savings clause preserving already-vested retirement rights (Section 3).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed CSC determination authority for edge cases (Section 2) and the preserved savings clause.

title: Executive Order 9155—Approving Regulations of the Civil Service Commission Relating to Efficiency-Rating Boards of Review

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A protective, due-process-EXPANDING order establishing a formal appeals mechanism for federal employees challenging efficiency ratings — matches this stream's established 'power-narrowing-reads-clean' pattern for orders that add rather than remove procedural protections.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Extensive genuine disclosed procedural safeguards: multi-member boards with management-designated, employee-elected, and CSC-designated chairman members; written appeal rights; optional oral hearings with representative rights; evidence exchange rights; majority-vote decisions with written reasoned notices; and mandatory reporting of decisions to the Civil Service Commission.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuinely protective; adds oversight and due-process rights rather than removing them.

title: Executive Order 9156—Further Defining the Functions and Duties of the Office of Defense Transportation

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Extends the already-scored Office of Defense Transportation's authority to all rubber-borne transportation facilities (passenger cars, buses, taxicabs, trucks), with broad 'develop programs' and 'formulate measures' authority amid the severe 1942 rubber-shortage crisis following the loss of Southeast Asian rubber supplies.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Federal departments/agencies with rubber-transportation functions 'shall conform' to the Director's prescribed policies, matching ODT's original well-networked coordinative structure (EO 8989).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Develop programs' and 'formulate measures' are broad, largely self-determined standards for this new subject-matter extension.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine, explicit disclosed boundary-preservation clause (Section 3) that this extension shall not limit the War Production Board's Chairman's authority nor the rationing authority already delegated to OPA.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable given the severe 1942 rubber-shortage crisis that directly led to nationwide gasoline rationing (primarily to conserve tires/rubber, not fuel).

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Explicitly coordinative and boundary-respecting toward the already-scored WPB/OPA institutional family.

title: Executive Order 9157—Regulations With Respect to the Making Available of Records, Schedules, Reports, Returns and Other Information by the Secretary of Commerce, and With Respect to the Use Thereof After the Same Have Been Made Available

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, procedural regulations governing how other agencies request Commerce Department records for war purposes, implementing Second War Powers Act Sec. 1402.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Extensive genuine disclosed procedural requirements: written requests must specify the requesting official, document description, delivery method, representative, and intended war-related use; the Secretary of Commerce retains disclosed authority to impose confidentiality-protecting conditions.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuinely protective/procedural; explicit disclosed carve-out (Section 6) preserving normal, non-confidential information-sharing practices; statutory criminal penalties for misuse are pre-existing (in the underlying Act), not newly created by this order.

title: Executive Order 9158—Air Medal

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Purely honorary/commendatory military decoration, matching the established EO 9050/9075 unit-citation treatment.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed award criteria and issuing authority.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9159—Amendment of Executive Order No. 7126, on Submission of Certain Estimates to the Bureau of the Budget

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An oversight-strengthening amendment newly subjecting eleven war-related government corporations (RFC subsidiaries, Defense Homes Corporation, etc.) to Bureau of the Budget administrative-expense estimate-submission requirements — adds a disclosed reporting check rather than removing one.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed itemized list of newly-covered entities.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Adds oversight rather than removing it.

title: Executive Order 9160—Amendment of Executive Order No. 8910 of September 27, 1941, Transferring Jurisdiction Over Certain Lands From the Secretary of Agriculture to the Secretary of the Navy

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, diff-aware amendment adding lands to the already-scored EO 8910 land-jurisdiction transfer (Indiana), implementing the existing Bankhead-Jones Act framework.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed diagram reference; genuine disclosed preservation of Agriculture's residual authority to complete pending land acquisitions.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 8910.

title: Executive Order 9161—Revoking Executive Order No. 8975 of December 13, 1941

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A pure deregulatory action revoking the prior unmitigated civil-service bypass authorization for Alien Property Division appointments (EO 8975, previously scored 42.86%) — reduces rather than creates structural weight, matching the established 'quiet regression/de-escalation' pattern.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed, complete revocation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Restores oversight rather than removing it, likely superseded by the Alien Property Custodian's own now-established hiring framework (EO 9095/9142).

title: Executive Order 9162—Designating the Ports of Guayanilla, Puerto Rico, and Jobos, Puerto Rico, as Customs Ports of Entry in Customs Collection District No. 49 (Puerto Rico)

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Routine administrative customs-port designation.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9163—Establishing a Women's Army Auxiliary Corps and Providing for Its Organization Into Units

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Implements explicit, contemporaneous congressional authorization (the WAAC Act, enacted the day before), with genuine disclosed numeric caps: no more than 100 units and 25,000 total enrollment, matching the National Guard federalization template's statutory-grounding-plus-disclosed-bound pattern.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed statutory basis and explicit enrollment/unit caps.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: The explicit disclosed numeric caps (100 units, 25,000 enrollment) function as a real, disclosed bound on this initial organizational step.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; implements explicit statutory authorization.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically significant as a landmark, enduring step in the integration of women into U.S. military service — though notably established only as an 'auxiliary' rather than full military status, itself a structural limitation later remedied by 1943's full militarization as the WAC.

title: Executive Order 9164—Amending Section 1 of Executive Order No. 8986 of December 19, 1941, Authorizing the Governor of the Panama Canal To Increase the Compensation of Certain Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow technical amendment to the already-scored EO 8986, notably ADDING a genuine disclosed sunset (war plus six months) that the original order lacked — an oversight-improving amendment.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed sunset newly added by this amendment.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 8986.

title: Executive Order 9165—Providing for the Protection of Essential Facilities From Sabotage and Other Destructive Acts

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Office of Civilian Defense is directed to establish a Facility Security Program with real coordinating/directive authority: it may 'review existing and proposed security plans and measures, and require the adoption of such additional measures as may be deemed necessary,' spanning nine named Federal agencies' assigned facility categories (communications, air/highway/rail transportation, public buildings, forests, minerals, gas/power utilities, water supply, foodstuffs).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed check: the Facility Security Program operates 'in conjunction with and subject to the approval of the Secretary of War'; authority is explicitly divided across nine named agencies rather than concentrated in OCD alone.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Specific facility-group assignments are disclosed for each of the nine named agencies, not left to a vague catch-all standard.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed non-preemption of FBI sabotage/espionage investigative jurisdiction (Section 5(b)) and explicit disclosed preservation of owners'/operators' primary protective responsibility (Section 5(a)).

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable as part of the comprehensive wartime critical-infrastructure protection apparatus developed amid 1942's acute sabotage anxiety, integrating with the Army, Navy, and Federal Power Commission's existing protective programs.

title: Executive Order 9166—Establishing the Lamesteer National Wildlife Refuge

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard, small (800-acre) wildlife-refuge template (Montana), subject to valid existing rights, with genuine disclosed hunting/trapping prohibitions subject to Secretary-prescribed exceptions.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed legal description.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9167—Establishing the Halfbreed Lake National Wildlife Refuge

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard, small (3,078.24-acre) wildlife-refuge template (Montana), subject to valid existing rights, with genuine disclosed hunting/trapping prohibitions subject to Secretary-prescribed exceptions.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed legal description; genuine disclosed private-land acquisition proviso.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9168—Establishing Matagorda Bay Defensive Sea Area

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Matches the established defensive-sea-area template (now administered by the Secretary of War/Army rather than Navy): vessels/persons violating regulations 'may be subjected to the force necessary to require compliance' and 'liable to prosecution' — a self-contained coercive enforcement mechanism.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed geographic boundaries.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing order tied to national defense, no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Secretary of War charged with disclosed publication and enforcement responsibility.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Part of the ongoing nationwide naval/army defensive-sea-area network.

title: Executive Order 9169—Possession Relinquished of Plants of Brewster Aeronautical Corporation

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A deregulatory reversion action relinquishing Navy possession of the Brewster plants seized under EO 9141, per the genuine disclosed termination standard already built into that order — reduces rather than creates structural weight.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed factual basis for the reversion determination.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Restores rather than removes oversight/ownership.

title: Executive Order 9170—Making Certain Navigation Laws of the United States Applicable to the Virgin Islands

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A well-bounded regulatory-extension order implementing existing statutory authority, with four genuine disclosed carve-out exceptions preserving local legislative authority over coastwise laws, certain older acts, small-vessel passenger-inspection requirements, and tonnage/entry fees.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed exceptions and statutory basis.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed carve-outs preserving local legislative authority.

title: Executive Order 9171—Enlarging the Naval Radio Station, Summit, Canal Zone

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, small (29.21-acre) diff-aware boundary amendment to the already-scored EO 7399 (Naval Radio Station, Canal Zone).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed metes-and-bounds description.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 7399.

title: Executive Order 9172—Establishing a Panel for the Creation of Emergency Boards for the Adjustment of Railway Labor Disputes

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Panel Chairman granted self-determined discretion ('in the judgment of the Chairman') to select three-member emergency boards, which then hold 'exclusive and final jurisdiction' over the referred dispute.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed nine-member Panel structure (not vested in a single official), operating within and adapting the existing Railway Labor Act statutory framework rather than creating an unrelated new mechanism.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed sunset: 'for the duration of the war and six months thereafter.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The 'exclusive and final jurisdiction' language mirrors the pre-existing Railway Labor Act Section 10 emergency-board finality provision rather than inventing new unchecked authority; the mechanism offers unions an alternative to strike votes while advancing wartime labor peace.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable given the wartime no-strike-pledge context and the railway labor-dispute-resolution system's importance to wartime logistics.

title: Executive Order 9173—Transferring the Control and Jurisdiction Over Certain Property Located at Juneau, Alaska, From the Department of the Interior to the Navy Department

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard, narrow inter-agency property-transfer order (Alaska dock) for Coast Guard use, subject to valid existing rights.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed metes-and-bounds description.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9174—Exemption of Harry P. Hoby from Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard one-year retirement-age exemption template.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed position, fixed one-year term.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9176—Transferring the Administration of the Act of June 8, 1938, as Amended, Requiring the Registration of Agents of Foreign Principals, From the Secretary of State to the Attorney General

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Pure inter-agency function transfer (Foreign Agents Registration Act administration) to an existing, accountable Cabinet official (Attorney General), implementing First War Powers Act reorganization authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed comment-sharing mechanism preserving the Secretary of State's foreign-relations input on registration statements (Section 3); genuine disclosed continuity of existing rules until modified.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed State Department comment mechanism preserved.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable given FARA's growing wartime importance (used extensively to track Axis propaganda agents) and the meaningful shift from diplomatic to prosecutorial administration of the registration regime.

title: Executive Order 9177—Defining Additional Functions, Duties and Powers of the Secretary of War, the Secretary of the Treasury, the Secretary of Agriculture, and the Reconstruction Finance Corporation

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Extends an existing 1914 Navy emergency-foreign-war-material-purchase and duty-free-entry authority to four more agencies (War, Treasury, Agriculture, RFC).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed Commissioner of Customs/Treasury certification-and-regulation requirement governing duty-free entry (Section 2).

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed Treasury regulatory check preserved.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A narrow, procedural customs-duty mechanism, not independently a landmark structural development.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Deemed necessary' remains a self-determined standard, though genuinely narrowed by the disclosed helium-program scope limitation.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war (Second War Powers Act duration), with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A narrow, procedural property-acquisition mechanism for a specifically bounded purpose, not independently a landmark development.

title: Executive Order 9180—Authorizing the Secretary of the Interior To Enter Into Contracts for the Disposal of Yucca Growing on the Public Domain

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed narrow purpose (strategic/critical material substitutes).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; narrowly bounded subject matter.

title: Executive Order 9181—Administration of the Federal Government Services in Alaska

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Section 5 requires civil Federal agencies operating in Alaska to 'conform with such requests as the Secretary of War may deem necessary' for effective wartime resource utilization — a broad subordination of numerous named civilian agencies' Alaska personnel, equipment, and facilities to military discretion.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed multi-member Alaska War Council structure (the Governor as Chairman, plus named departmental representatives from Interior, Agriculture, Commerce, FSA, FWA, an Attorney General designee, and an elected resident) provides real consultative structure.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As the Secretary of War may deem necessary' is a self-determined standard governing civilian-agency conformance requests.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to First War Powers Act Title I's duration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed liaison-officer structure channels military-civilian coordination through the Council rather than ad hoc military dictate; a genuine disclosed confidentiality carve-out (Section 3) also limits what flows even to the President outside military channels.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically significant: issued amid Alaska's active combat status, essentially simultaneous with the June 1942 Japanese invasion and occupation of Attu and Kiska in the Aleutian Islands.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Section 5's broad civilian-agency-conformance requirement represents a real, disclosed subordination of numerous named civilian departments' and agencies' Alaska operations to Secretary of War discretion.

title: Executive Order 9182—Consolidating Certain War Information Functions Into an Office of War Information

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Director is granted broad, self-determined, BINDING directive authority over all Federal departments' and agencies' war-information activities, including power to review, clear, and approve all federal radio and motion-picture programs and to 'require the curtailment or elimination of any Federal information service, program, or release which he deems to be wasteful.'

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed Committee on War Information Policy (representatives of State, War, Navy, the Joint Psychological Warfare Committee, and the Coordinator of Inter-American Affairs) providing consultative structure that the Director must consult before acting; genuine disclosed geographic jurisdictional carve-out (Section 6) preserving the Coordinator of Inter-American Affairs' separate Western Hemisphere authority.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single coherent subject (wartime public information) despite absorbing four predecessor functions.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As he may deem necessary or appropriate' and 'he deems to be wasteful' are largely self-determined standards.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed Committee-consultation requirement, mandatory disclosed collaboration with the Director of Censorship and the Defense Communications Board, and the disclosed geographic carve-out preserving another agency's separate jurisdiction.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically very significant: OWI became the primary WWII propaganda and public-information agency, playing a major and often controversial role in shaping wartime public opinion and information policy — a natural institutional companion to the already-CRITICAL EO 8985 Office of Censorship.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Explicitly abolishes the Division of Information (Section 10) and fully absorbs the already-scored Office of Facts and Figures (EO 8922) and the Office of Government Reports, plus significant portions of the Coordinator of Information's functions — a substantial multi-agency consolidation event.

title: Executive Order 9183—Changing the Name of the Defense Communications Board To Board of War Communications

flags.justification: Purely nominal renaming of the already-scored Defense Communications Board (EO 8546/8964/9089 institutional family); grants no new authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion created; substantive authority remains exactly as previously scored.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Tied to 'the continuance of any of the wars in which the United States is now engaged,' with no independently fixed expiration.

title: Executive Order 9189—Suspending Certain Statutory Provisions Relating to Employment in the Canal Zone

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A comprehensive but genuinely well-designed and protective rewrite of regulations governing the temporary detail of qualified U.S. employees to other American-republic governments, the Philippines, and Liberia, implementing existing statutory authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Extensive genuine disclosed procedural safeguards: formal foreign-government request requirement, State Department determination process, a genuine disclosed compensation cap (no more than 50% additional compensation), disclosed reimbursement/trust-fund mechanisms, mandatory letters of instruction, and an explicit restriction against unauthorized services or compensation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuinely protective, extensively disclosed procedural framework preserved throughout.

title: Executive Order 9191—Amending the Foreign Service Regulations of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical amendment adding the War and Navy Departments to an existing numbered list within the Foreign Service Regulations, renumbering subsequent items accordingly.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed narrow technical amendment.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion created.

title: Executive Order 9192—Amending Executive Order No. 9140 of April 20, 1942, Establishing the Safford National Wildlife Refuge

flags.name: Power Concentration

flags.name: Accountability Gaps

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 9140.

title: Executive Order 9193—Amending Executive Order No. 9095 Establishing the Office of Alien Property Custodian and Defining Its Functions and Duties and Related Matters

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A comprehensive rewrite/expansion of the already-scored EO 9095, granting the Alien Property Custodian extraordinarily broad, self-determined power to 'direct, manage, supervise, control or vest' enemy and foreign-national business enterprises, patents, ships, and property nationwide, paired with an explicit anti-judicial-review clause (Section 12): 'No person affected by any order... shall be entitled to challenge the validity thereof' — a critical, unchecked concentration of power over private property with no disclosed court recourse.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Some genuine disclosed checks: mandatory State Department consultation before vesting property (Section 11); genuine disclosed Board of Economic Warfare representative-designation requirement (Section 7); genuine disclosed Treasury/Custodian mutual information-sharing duty (Section 4).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As he deems necessary in the national interest' is a broad, self-determined standard governing the Custodian's core seizure/vesting authority throughout.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Section 12's explicit denial of any right to challenge the validity of Custodian/Treasury orders, plus Section 13's presumption that consultation/certification requirements were satisfied, together constitute a stark, disclosed preemption of judicial and administrative oversight over an extraordinarily consequential property-seizure power.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically very significant: this is the operative framework under which the U.S. government seized and administered billions of dollars in Axis-owned and enemy-national property during WWII, a major and (in the postwar period) legally contested exercise of executive power over private property — notably an escalation over the original EO 9095's already-substantial (50%) score, given the more elaborated vesting authority and more emphatic anti-review language in this rewrite.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed, highly detailed qualifying criteria and numeric flight-hour/time thresholds.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; purely implements existing statutory pay formula.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable as the primary WWII-era charitable-oversight regulatory apparatus, touching a wide swath of civil-society fundraising activity nationwide.

title: Executive Order 9216—Authorizing the Adjutant General To Execute Certificates of Facts or Events Officially Recorded When it is Contrary to Public Policy to Divulge the Source of Official Knowledge or the Text of the Official Record

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A self-determined 'contrary to public policy' trigger permits the Adjutant General to certify facts as prima facie evidence in courts-martial while withholding the underlying evidentiary source or record text — a real, disclosed accountability-reducing evidentiary shortcut.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Narrow scope, limited to the Manual for Courts-Martial evidence-procedure amendment.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Contrary to public policy' is a vague, self-determined standard governing when the underlying source may be withheld.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Reduces courts-martial defendants' ability to examine or challenge the underlying evidentiary basis of officially certified facts — a genuine, if narrow, preemption of evidentiary scrutiny.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A narrow military-evidentiary-procedure matter, though notable as an early instance of wartime secrecy practice reaching into judicial evidence rules.

flags.name: Power Concentration

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Deemed necessary' is a brief, self-determined standard with no further criteria disclosed.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war (Second War Powers Act duration), with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A narrow, procedural property-acquisition mechanism, not independently a landmark development.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed procedural safeguards and merit-based selection criteria preserved throughout.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A protective civil-service merit-system reform, not a landmark concentration of power.

title: Executive Order 9242-A—Amending Executive Order No. 9158 of May 11, 1942, To Provide That the Air Medal May Be Awarded to Persons Serving With the Army, Navy, Marine Corps, or Coast Guard of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Note: the local corpus text stored under EO-9242 corresponds to a differently-numbered, unrelated order (a narrow Procurement Division optical-glass authorization also dated September 11, 1942) — a corpus/DB numbering mismatch of the same '-A' suffix type previously encountered with EO 9132-A and EO 9153-A. This record is coded by title-pattern-matching: the disclosed title indicates a narrow, technical amendment expanding an existing military decoration's (Air Medal) award eligibility to additional service branches, directly matching the established narrow military-decoration-eligibility template (e.g., EO 9265 campaign medals) already scored at 0% throughout this stream.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects

title: Executive Order 9244—Amending Executive Order No. 9054 Entitled "Establishing a War Shipping Administration in the Executive Office of the President and Defining Its Functions and Duties"

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A substantial diff-aware expansion of the already-CRITICAL-scored War Shipping Administration (EO 9054, 56.25%): grants the Administrator full exercise of the Maritime Commission's property acquisition/requisition powers (drawn from EO 8942/9138/9129), the Commission's contract/audit functions (EO 9001/9127), and Suits in Admiralty Act authority — a further significant concentration of already-critical wartime shipping authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Matches the original EO 9054's disclosed statutory-framework grounding; the amendment operates within the pre-existing referenced Executive Orders and Acts rather than creating wholly new unchecked authority.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Self-determined 'as the Administrator may deem necessary' standards continue to govern exercise of the newly consolidated powers.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The Suits in Admiralty Act explicitly preserves a judicial-review pathway for admiralty claims arising from these functions.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically significant as a further consolidation of wartime shipping, procurement, and property authority into a single Administrator already possessing 'final decision' powers.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Explicitly absorbs Maritime Commission functions across multiple domains (contracts, audits, property acquisition, requisition, admiralty suits) into the WSA Administrator — a substantial multi-order consolidation.

title: Executive Order 9247—Transferring Certain Employment Service and Training Functions to the War Manpower Commission

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed preservation of NYA and Apprenticeship Training Service as organizational entities within WMC (institutional-continuity check); genuine disclosed coordination requirement preserving unemployment-compensation agencies' access to Employment Service data and facilities (Section 4).

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles four distinct, substantively different programs (employment placement, youth training, apprenticeship, industry training) into a single consolidation order.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Operates within the already-scored EO 9139 WMC accountability structure.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically significant as a major consolidation of New Deal-era youth and training infrastructure (notably the National Youth Administration) into the wartime manpower apparatus.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Explicit multi-agency, multi-program consolidation and absorption into the War Manpower Commission.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
