flags.justification: Clear, disclosed eligibility criteria (service areas, dates).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9267—Suspending Until Further Order the Maximum Limitations of Punishment for Violation of Article of War 61

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A self-determined removal of an existing punishment ceiling for AWOL offenses, effectively granting courts-martial authorities discretion to impose punishments up to the full statutory maximum without the Table of Maximum Punishments' moderating limitation.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Narrow scope, limited to Article of War 61 (AWOL) offenses only.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Until further order' is an open-ended, self-determined duration with no stated criteria for reinstatement of the limitation.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: 'Until further order,' no fixed end date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Actively removes a disclosed pre-existing procedural check (the Table of Maximum Punishments ceiling) rather than merely declining to add one — matches the 'quiet regression' pattern already established with EO 9138.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable given the significant increase in courts-martial punitive authority over AWOL offenses during a period of acute wartime manpower strain.

title: Executive Order 9268—Suspending in Part the Provisions of Section 12 of the Naval Aviation Cadet Act of 1942

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Suspends unspecified statutory provisions of Section 12 of the Naval Aviation Cadet Act (excepting the first proviso) for a defined category of formerly-enlisted officers, based on self-determined wartime authority; the underlying statutory text is not reproduced in this brief order, limiting independent assessment of its full scope.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Tied to 'the present war,' with no independently fixed expiration.

title: Executive Order 9269—Modifying the Limitation on Fixed Fees With Respect to Contracts of the Treasury Department for Architectural and Engineering Services

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, bounding amendment: replaces a blanket fee-limitation exemption with a specific disclosed cap (4% of estimated project cost) for Treasury architectural/engineering contracts.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed numeric cap.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed numeric ceiling constrains the fee-setting discretion.

title: Executive Order 9270—Correcting Executive Order No. 9257 of October 15, 1942, Enlarging Naval Petroleum Reserve No. 1

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Pure technical correction to a land-description error in the already-scored EO 9257.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed correction.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 9257.

title: Executive Order 9272—Extension of Trust Periods on Indian Lands Expiring During Calendar Year 1943

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A genuinely protective, standard periodic extension of federal trust protections for Indian lands, preventing lapse into unrestricted fee status; matches established prior similar orders in this corpus.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope and fixed 25-year extension term; genuine disclosed carve-out preserving Congress's own reserved authority over specific lands.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Fixed 25-year extension term.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; genuinely protective of beneficiaries' land rights.

title: Executive Order 9271—Exemption of George T. Summerlin From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Named-individual retirement-age exemption, with a genuine disclosed outer bound (not beyond November 30, 1947) despite open-worded 'for such time as may be deemed necessary' phrasing.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed position, outer-capped term.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed outer cap functions as a real bound.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9273—Amendment of Executive Order No. 8910 of September 27, 1941, and Executive Order No. 9160 of May 11, 1941, Transferring Jurisdiction Over Certain Lands From the Secretary of Agriculture to the Secretary of the Navy

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, diff-aware amendment adding additional acquired/in-process lands to already-scored predecessor land-transfer orders (Indiana), with genuine disclosed pending-acquisition jurisdiction retention.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed area descriptions.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 8910/9160.

title: Executive Order 9274—Authorizing an Increase in the Number of Units and Members of the Women's Army Auxiliary Corps

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Diff-aware amendment implementing a specific contemporaneous congressional amendment (Oct. 26, 1942 Act) to the already-scored EO 9163 WAAC framework, with a genuine disclosed numeric cap (150,000, up sixfold from the original 25,000) that continues to function as a real, disclosed bound.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed statutory basis and cap.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: The numeric cap remains a real, disclosed bound.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 9163, implements explicit statutory authorization.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable as reflecting the WAAC's much-greater-than-anticipated wartime expansion.

title: Executive Order 9276—Establishing the Petroleum Administration for War and Defining Its Functions and Duties

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Petroleum Administrator (Secretary of the Interior ex officio) is granted broad, self-determined authority to establish basic policies and issue binding operating directives to the entire U.S. petroleum industry.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed check: the Administrator's core enforcement power operates 'subject to the direction of the Chairman of the War Production Board' (Section 3(d)); an unusually extensive, explicit multi-agency non-preemption clause (Section 9) expressly preserves War/Navy secret-military-necessity authority, State Department foreign-policy authority, ICC statutory powers, Federal Power Commission Natural Gas Act authority, Board of Economic Warfare authority, and OPA price-control authority.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles petroleum production planning, refining, distribution, pricing consultation, rationing consultation, pipeline-construction review, and international petroleum liaison functions into a single office, though coherently organized around the single petroleum-industry subject.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As the Administrator may deem necessary' appears repeatedly as a self-determined standard for enforcement directives.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The extraordinarily extensive, explicit Section 9 carve-out list is a genuine, disclosed preemption-of-oversight safeguard rare in its breadth and explicitness among War Production Board-family orders.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically very significant: became the central wartime petroleum-policy institution overseeing the industry through the war's most intense mobilization period, including major pipeline projects.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Explicitly abolishes the Office of the Petroleum Coordinator for War and absorbs its functions, plus absorbs petroleum-related duties previously assigned to the Facility Security Program (EO 9165).

title: Executive Order 9277—Award of the Purple Heart to Persons Serving with the Navy, Marine Corps or Coast Guard of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard military-decoration-eligibility extension template, matching the parallel Army regulation.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed eligibility criteria.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9278—Appointment of Benjamin W. Thoron, Director, Division of Territories and Island Possessions, Department of the Interior, as the Administrator of the Puerto Rico Reconstruction Administration

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, named personnel appointment implementing an already-existing statutory administrative framework, no new authority granted.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed statutory basis.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in the predecessor orders establishing the PRRA.

title: Executive Order 9280—Delegating Authority with Respect to the Nation's Food Program

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Secretary of Agriculture is granted 'full responsibility for and control over the Nation's food program,' including explicitly disclosed subpoena/audit power (Title XIII Second War Powers Act), priorities and allocation power over all food nationwide reaching every level of distribution down to retail sale to consumers, requisitioning power, property-acquisition power, plant-takeover power, and anti-trust-prosecution power — an extraordinarily broad, multi-domain concentration of authority over the entire national food supply chain from production through consumer retail sale.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed checks: a mandatory advisory committee (State/War/Navy/Lend-Lease/Board of Economic Warfare/War Production Board representatives) must be consulted before food allocations; genuine disclosed WPB-Secretary joint-determination requirement for shortage-allocation disputes, escalating to the President if they disagree (Section 3); genuine disclosed OPA consultation requirement before civilian-rationing decisions (Section 4); genuine disclosed non-limitation of the Price Administrator's and Economic Stabilization Director's existing authority (Section 14).

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles an extraordinary range of distinct powers (production planning, allocation, procurement, distribution, civilian-rationing execution via OPA, requisitioning, property acquisition, plant operation/seizure, anti-trust prosecution, subpoena/audit) into a single office — one of the most extensively bundled orders in the entire stream.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As he shall determine necessary or desirable' and 'in the public interest' are broad self-determined standards governing exercise of the core allocation and procurement powers.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed dispute-escalation-to-the-President mechanism (Sections 3 and 11) for inter-agency conflicts; genuine disclosed non-limitation clauses expressly preserving OPA and Economic Stabilization Director authority (Section 14).

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically very significant: created the operative framework for the wartime Food Production Administration and Food Distribution Administration, controlling the entire U.S. food supply and civilian food-rationing system during the war's peak — one of the most consequential and far-reaching domestic-economic-control orders of the war, touching every American household's food access.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Explicitly abolishes the War Production Board's Food Requirements Committee and consolidates the Agricultural Adjustment Administration, Farm Credit Administration, Farm Security Administration, and multiple Bureau of Agricultural Economics/Agricultural War Relations functions into two newly-created agencies (Food Production Administration, Food Distribution Administration) within the Department of Agriculture.

title: Executive Order 9281—Inspection of Income, Excess-Profits, Declared Value Excess-Profits, and Capital Stock Tax Returns by the Special Committee on Un-American Activities, House of Representatives

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Grants a congressional investigative committee (the predecessor to HUAC) broad access to a decade-plus of corporate tax returns across multiple Revenue Acts, for the stated purpose of carrying out a broadly-worded House Resolution investigative mandate.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed Treasury regulatory compliance requirement governing the inspection.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Treasury regulatory constraint preserved; this is an inter-branch (executive-to-legislative) information grant rather than an executive concentration of power.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable given the Special Committee on Un-American Activities' later controversial role in domestic political investigations; this early broad tax-return-access grant is a notable precedent.

title: Executive Order 9282—Transferring Jurisdiction Over Certain Lands From the Secretary of Agriculture to the Secretary of War for Military Purposes

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Matches the established Agriculture-to-War land-transfer-for-military-purposes template (Oklahoma, Cookson Hills project), with a genuine disclosed automatic-reversion clause; diff-aware, explicitly supersedes a 1939 order and revokes a 1940 Indian-benefit transfer.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed area description.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed reversion to Agriculture 'when no longer needed for military purposes.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; automatic-reversion mechanism preserved.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
